phrases.intent_en: Ask when the Chinese Garden lantern festival is
phrases.text_local: Le festival des lanternes du jardin chinois, c'est à quelle période ?
phrases.pronunciation: luh fes-tee-VAHL day lan-TURN doo zhar-DAN shee-NWAH say ah kel pay-ree-OD
phrases.cultural_note: The Magic of Lanterns festival in the Chinese Garden runs September–October and is one of Montréal's most spectacular autumn events.

responses.english: In September, check the dates on the website.
responses.phonetic: ahn sep-TAHM-bruh vay-ree-FYAY lay DAHT soor luh SEET
responses.local: En septembre, vérifiez les dates sur le site.

phrases.intent_en: Ask which greenhouse has the tropical plants
phrases.text_local: Quelle serre contient les plantes tropicales ?
phrases.pronunciation: kel SAIR kon-tee-YAN lay PLAHNT tro-pee-KAHL
phrases.cultural_note: The botanical garden has 10 exhibition greenhouses. The First Nations Garden and the Japanese Garden are also highlights.

responses.english: The tropical greenhouse is over there.
responses.phonetic: lah SAIR troh-pee-KAHL eh par lah-BAH
responses.local: La serre tropicale est par là-bas.

phrases.intent_en: Ask for a map of the gardens
phrases.text_local: Est-ce que je pourrais avoir un plan du jardin ?
phrases.pronunciation: ess-KUH zhuh poo-REH ah-VWAHR un PLAHN doo zhar-DAN
phrases.cultural_note: Free maps are available at the entrance pavilion. The garden covers 75 hectares with 22,000 plant species.

responses.english: Yes, pick one up at reception.
responses.phonetic: wee pruh-NAY zahn uhn ah lah-KUY

phrases.intent_en: Ask if the garden café is open
phrases.text_local: Le café du jardin est ouvert en ce moment ?
phrases.pronunciation: luh kah-FAY doo zhar-DAN ay oo-VAIR on suh mo-MAH
phrases.cultural_note: The garden café serves seasonal menus featuring local produce. It's a lovely spot for lunch amid the greenery.

phrases.intent_en: Ask how to get to the Japanese Garden
phrases.text_local: C'est par où pour aller au jardin japonais ?
phrases.pronunciation: say par OO poor ah-LAY oh zhar-DAN zhah-po-NEH
phrases.cultural_note: The Japanese Garden was designed by Japanese landscape architect Ken Nakajima and features a traditional tea house.

responses.english: Follow the signs, it is five minutes away.
responses.phonetic: swee-VAY lay pah-NOH say tah SAHNK mee-NOOT
responses.local: Suivez les panneaux, c'est à cinq minutes.
